message:
chore(beads): sync issue tracker

Update .beads/issues.jsonl with latest issue metadata changes and
status updates from concurrent agent work sessions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -11,13 +11,14 @@
 {"id":"bd-1zid","title":"TOON: Add BV_OUTPUT_FORMAT env var support","description":"Add environment variable support for format selection.

## Precedence
1. CLI flag `--format toon` (highest)
2. `BV_OUTPUT_FORMAT=toon`
3. `TOON_DEFAULT_FORMAT=toon`
4. Default: json

## Implementation
```go
func getRobotFormat() string {
    if format := os.Getenv("BV_OUTPUT_FORMAT"); format != "" {
        return format
    }
    if format := os.Getenv("TOON_DEFAULT_FORMAT"); format != "" {
        return format
    }
    return "json"
}
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T07:19:16.872852012Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T07:06:42.357129978Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T07:06:42.357112285Z","close_reason":"Already implemented - BV_OUTPUT_FORMAT env var is checked in resolveRobotOutputFormat at main.go:6787","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

-{"id":"bd-23lq","title":"Fix Solarized Dark / non-truecolor terminal color compatibility (GH #101)","description":"## Context
GitHub issue #101 filed by mhpaler (2026-02-07). On macOS Terminal.app with Solarized Dark profile, the right-hand detail pane is rendered with a solid ANSI Green (#2) background making text unreadable. Bottom status bar also has low-contrast color blocks.

## Root Cause Analysis
The codebase uses hex color values everywhere (Dracula theme: #50FA7B for green, #282A36 for dark bg, #1A3D2A for green badge bg). However, Charmbracelet's colorprofile package has a Convert method that downgrades colors: TrueColor → ANSI256 → ANSI (16 colors). When the color profile detects ANSI (16-color):
1. ansi.Convert16(c) maps #50FA7B → ANSI Green (slot #2)
2. In Solarized Dark, ANSI Green (slot #2) is a bright green background color
3. Several background fills use green-adjacent hex colors that all degrade to ANSI Green as background
4. The glamour markdown renderer's docBgPtr is hardcoded to #282a36 which also gets degraded

## Owner Response
Confirmed the issue. Acknowledged that TUI code uses hardcoded ANSI color indices for pane backgrounds.

## Fix Options
1. Use terminal default background (no explicit bg) for pane fills when color profile is < TrueColor
2. Detect color profile at startup and skip background fills in ANSI (16-color) mode
3. Add --no-bg or --theme CLI flag for non-standard palettes
4. Set glamour markdown docBgPtr to nil (no background) when not truecolor

## Key Files
- pkg/ui/theme.go (hex colors via lipgloss.AdaptiveColor)
- pkg/ui/styles.go (background colors: ColorBgDark #1E1F29, ColorBgSubtle #363949, etc.)
- pkg/ui/markdown.go (buildStyleFromTheme(), docBgPtr = #282a36)
- pkg/ui/model.go (lines 5422-5901, status bar extensive background colors)
- vendor/github.com/charmbracelet/colorprofile/profile.go (Convert method, degradation path)

## Considerations
- This is a real bug affecting users on popular terminal configurations (Solarized is very widely used)
- The fix touches styling across multiple files — needs careful testing
- Must not break the experience for truecolor terminal users (most users)
- Consider detecting the color profile once at startup and choosing a style strategy","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:17:38.046656489Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T18:17:38.046656489Z","external_ref":"gh:Dicklesworthstone/beads_viewer#101","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bug","color","terminal-compat","ui"]}
+{"id":"bd-23lq","title":"Fix Solarized Dark / non-truecolor terminal color compatibility (GH #101)","description":"## Context
GitHub issue #101 filed by mhpaler (2026-02-07). On macOS Terminal.app with Solarized Dark profile, the right-hand detail pane is rendered with a solid ANSI Green (#2) background making text unreadable. Bottom status bar also has low-contrast color blocks.

## Root Cause Analysis
The codebase uses hex color values everywhere (Dracula theme: #50FA7B for green, #282A36 for dark bg, #1A3D2A for green badge bg). Charmbracelet colorprofile package Convert() method downgrades colors: TrueColor → ANSI256 → ANSI (16 colors). When the terminal is ANSI (16-color):
1. ansi.Convert16(c) maps #50FA7B → ANSI Green (slot #2)
2. In Solarized Dark, ANSI Green (slot #2) is a bright green background color
3. Multiple background fills use green-adjacent hex colors that all degrade to ANSI Green as background
4. The glamour markdown renderer docBgPtr is hardcoded to #282a36 which also gets degraded

## Chosen Approach: Color-profile-aware styling
At startup, detect the terminal color profile using colorprofile.Detect(). Then:
1. If TrueColor or ANSI256: use current Dracula hex colors (no change for most users)
2. If ANSI (16) or below: use terminal default bg (no explicit bg), use ANSI foreground colors that work across all palettes (white, default, bold)
3. For glamour markdown: set docBgPtr to nil (transparent) when not TrueColor

This approach preserves the beautiful Dracula look for TrueColor users (the majority) while making the app usable on 16-color terminals.

## Implementation Plan

### Step 1: Add color profile detection to theme.go
- Import colorprofile package
- Detect profile once at init time
- Export a TermProfile variable

### Step 2: Update styles.go background colors
- For ANSI mode: replace all Background() calls with conditional logic
- Use lipgloss.NoColor{} for backgrounds when profile < TrueColor
- Keep foreground colors but use ANSI-safe palette (e.g., lipgloss.Color("7") for white)

### Step 3: Fix markdown.go glamour rendering
- Set docBgPtr to nil when profile < TrueColor
- This makes markdown render with transparent bg, using terminal default

### Step 4: Update model.go status bar
- Lines 5422-5901 have extensive bg color usage in the status bar
- Apply same conditional: skip bg colors for ANSI mode
- Use bold/dim instead of color for visual hierarchy

## Key Files
- pkg/ui/theme.go (add profile detection, conditional color functions)
- pkg/ui/styles.go (conditional backgrounds)
- pkg/ui/markdown.go (conditional docBgPtr)
- pkg/ui/model.go (status bar colors, lines 5422-5901)
- vendor/github.com/charmbracelet/colorprofile (reference for API)

## Testing
- Test on both TrueColor (iTerm2/WezTerm) and ANSI (macOS Terminal with Solarized)
- Verify no visual regression for TrueColor users
- Verify readability on Solarized Dark/Light, Monokai, default terminal profiles
- Use TERM=xterm (forces 16-color) for local testing

## Considerations
- Must NOT break the experience for TrueColor users (most users)
- Solarized is very widely used; this is a real usability bug
- The colorprofile package is already vendored, so no new deps
- Consider adding a --no-bg or --theme CLI flag for users who want explicit control","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:17:38.046656489Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T18:59:32.791112545Z","external_ref":"gh:Dicklesworthstone/beads_viewer#101","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bug","color","terminal-compat","ui"],"comments":[{"id":68,"issue_id":"bd-23lq","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"IMPLEMENTATION OPTIMIZATION: To minimize code touches across 4 files (theme.go, styles.go, markdown.go, model.go lines 5422-5901), create centralized helper functions in theme.go:

var TermProfile colorprofile.Profile // detected once at init

func ThemeBg(hex string) lipgloss.TerminalColor {
    if TermProfile < colorprofile.TrueColor { return lipgloss.NoColor{} }
    return lipgloss.Color(hex)
}

func ThemeFg(hex string) lipgloss.TerminalColor {
    if TermProfile < colorprofile.ANSI256 { return lipgloss.ANSIColor(7) } // white
    return lipgloss.Color(hex)
}

Then replace direct hex references with ThemeBg()/ThemeFg() calls. This centralizes the conditional logic in ONE place rather than adding if-statements across hundreds of lines in model.go. If the profile changes (e.g., user sets TERM after startup), only the helpers need updating.","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:59:32Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-29b3","title":"Profile + opportunity matrix (identify top hotspots)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T19:26:33.601983923Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T21:59:43.034124196Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T21:59:43.034077237Z","close_reason":"Completed: Created OPPORTUNITY_MATRIX.md with 12 optimization opportunities identified from CPU/memory profiling. Document includes detailed analysis, implementation roadmap, and success criteria.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-29b3","depends_on_id":"bd-20y5","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-01-21T19:26:33.631753288Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-29yy","title":"Profile graph-build allocations (Gonum build path)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T17:29:44.927865045Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T18:01:11.802831432Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T18:01:11.802742084Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-29yy","depends_on_id":"bd-3c0m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T17:29:45.228769634Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":63,"issue_id":"bd-29yy","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Graph-Build Profiling Complete

### Memory Allocation Breakdown (874MB total for 951 iterations)
| Component | Flat | % | Cumulative |
|-----------|------|---|------------|
| NewAnalyzer | 369MB | 42% | 749MB (86%) |
| DirectedGraph.SetEdge | 263MB | 30% | 263MB |
| DirectedGraph.AddNode | 53MB | 6% | 90MB |
| bufio.NewReaderSize | 50MB | 6% | 50MB |
| set.Ints.Add | 37MB | 4% | 37MB |
| DirectedGraph.NewEdge | 23MB | 3% | 23MB |

### CPU Breakdown
| Component | Cumulative % |
|-----------|-------------|
| NewAnalyzer | 61% |
| DirectedGraph.SetEdge | 27% |
| mapassign_fast64 | 24% |
| mallocgc | 19% |
| GC overhead | 14% |
| DirectedGraph.AddNode | 9% |

### Root Cause
Gonum's DirectedGraph uses **map-backed edge sets** which cause:
1. Heavy allocation in SetEdge (263MB)
2. Map grow/rehash overhead (17% CPU in table.grow)
3. GC pressure from small allocations

### Recommended Optimization (for bd-1wse)
Replace Gonum's DirectedGraph with a compact adjacency-list implementation:
- Pre-allocate node array at known size
- Use []int64 adjacency lists instead of map-backed sets
- Eliminates map overhead entirely

Benchmark: 735KB/op, 4647 allocs/op for 696 issues
","created_at":"2026-01-21T18:01:04Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2ew5","title":"TOON: Add --format flag and Format enum (pkg/robot/format.go)","description":"Add output format infrastructure.

## Files
- `pkg/robot/format.go` (NEW)
- `cmd/bv/main.go` (modify flag definitions)

## Implementation

```go
type Format string

const (
    FormatJSON Format = "json"
    FormatTOON Format = "toon"
    FormatAuto Format = "auto"
)

func Encode(payload any, format Format, w io.Writer) error {
    switch format {
    case FormatTOON:
        output, err := encodeToon(payload)
        if err != nil {
            return err
        }
        _, err = w.Write([]byte(output))
        return err
    case FormatAuto:
        // Try TOON, fallback to JSON
    default:
        return json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(payload)
    }
}
```

## CLI Flag
```go
outputFormat := flag.String("format", "",
    "Structured output format for --robot-* commands: json or toon")
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T07:17:45.188873455Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T07:06:21.449513971Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T07:06:21.449495165Z","close_reason":"Already implemented - --format flag exists in main.go:59, Format enum handled via string constant","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2juk","title":"TOON: Update newRobotEncoder for format support","description":"Modify `newRobotEncoder` to accept format parameter.

## File
`cmd/bv/main.go` (line ~6583)

## Current
```go
func newRobotEncoder(w io.Writer) *json.Encoder {
    encoder := json.NewEncoder(w)
    if os.Getenv("BV_PRETTY_JSON") == "1" {
        encoder.SetIndent("", "  ")
    }
    return encoder
}
```

## New
```go
func newRobotEncoder(w io.Writer, format string) *robotEncoder {
    return &robotEncoder{
        w:      w,
        format: Format(format),
        pretty: os.Getenv("BV_PRETTY_JSON") == "1",
    }
}
```

## Impact
35+ callsites need updating from:
`encoder := newRobotEncoder(os.Stdout)`
to:
`encoder := newRobotEncoder(os.Stdout, *robotFormat)`","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T07:18:29.261346652Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T07:06:25.226203135Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T07:06:25.226183929Z","close_reason":"Already implemented - newRobotEncoder at main.go:6780 switches on robotOutputFormat for toon/json","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2kxo","title":"TASK: bv --robot-schema output for JSON Schema","description":"Problem: agents lack a canonical JSON schema for robot outputs, which hurts validation and tool integration.

Goal:
- Add `--robot-schema` (or `bv robot-docs schemas`) that emits JSON Schema for each robot command output.
- Provide a top-level map keyed by command name (robot-triage, robot-plan, etc.).
- Include schema versioning and link to output envelope.

Acceptance:
- Command returns JSON/TOON only on stdout.
- Schemas cover at least: robot-triage, robot-next, robot-plan, robot-insights, robot-priority, robot-graph.
- README/robot-help updated with usage example.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T10:09:58.036791807Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T07:05:41.843764095Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T07:05:41.843734840Z","close_reason":"Implemented --robot-schema with JSON Schema output for all robot commands","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["agent-friendly","robot","schema"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2kxo","depends_on_id":"bd-c5g","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-01-25T10:09:58.036791807Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2lmf","title":"TOON: Add Go unit tests for format encoding","description":"Unit tests for TOON encoding.

## File
`pkg/robot/toon_test.go`

## Test Cases
```go
func TestTriageResultTOON(t *testing.T) {
    triage := analysis.TriageResult{...}
    output, err := encodeToon(triage)
    require.NoError(t, err)
}

func TestTabularArray(t *testing.T) {
    recs := []analysis.Recommendation{{...}, {...}}
    output, err := encodeToon(recs)
    require.NoError(t, err)
    require.Contains(t, output, "[2]{")
}

func TestRoundTrip(t *testing.T) {
    // Encode to TOON, decode with tru, compare
}
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T07:21:30.761349396Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T07:08:58.124558723Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T07:08:58.124538205Z","close_reason":"Added 4 comprehensive TOON unit tests: TestTOONOutputFormat, TestTOONRoundTrip, TestTOONTokenStats, TestTOONSchemaOutput","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-2rih","title":"Test: Solarized and non-TrueColor terminal color compatibility","description":"## What
Test suite and manual verification script for the color compatibility fix (bd-23lq).

## Automated Tests (Go unit tests)

### pkg/ui/theme_test.go (NEW or MODIFY existing)
- TestColorProfile_Detection: verify Detect() returns a valid profile
- TestConditionalBgColor_TrueColor: when profile=TrueColor, returns hex color
- TestConditionalBgColor_ANSI: when profile=ANSI, returns NoColor
- TestConditionalFgColor_TrueColor: returns hex foreground
- TestConditionalFgColor_ANSI: returns ANSI-safe color index

### pkg/ui/styles_test.go
- TestStylesBgTransparent_ANSI: verify no explicit bg in ANSI mode
- TestStylesBgPresent_TrueColor: verify bg colors present in TrueColor mode

### pkg/ui/markdown_test.go
- TestGlamourDocBg_TrueColor: docBgPtr = #282a36
- TestGlamourDocBg_ANSI: docBgPtr = nil (transparent)

## Manual Test Script (scripts/test_color_compat.sh)
```bash
#\!/usr/bin/env bash
# Test bv rendering under different color profiles
# Run this on a machine with bv binary available

log() { printf "[%s] [%s] %s\n" "$(date -Iseconds)" "$1" "$2"; }

# Test 1: Force 16-color mode
log "TEST_1" "Starting: TERM=xterm (16 colors)"
TERM=xterm bv --robot-triage > /dev/null 2>&1 && log "TEST_1" "PASS: No crash in 16-color mode" || log "TEST_1" "FAIL: Crash in 16-color mode"

# Test 2: Force 256-color mode
log "TEST_2" "Starting: TERM=xterm-256color"
TERM=xterm-256color bv --robot-triage > /dev/null 2>&1 && log "TEST_2" "PASS: No crash in 256-color mode" || log "TEST_2" "FAIL: Crash in 256-color mode"

# Test 3: TrueColor mode (default)
log "TEST_3" "Starting: COLORTERM=truecolor"
COLORTERM=truecolor bv --robot-triage > /dev/null 2>&1 && log "TEST_3" "PASS: No crash in TrueColor mode" || log "TEST_3" "FAIL: Crash in TrueColor mode"

# Test 4: Dumb terminal
log "TEST_4" "Starting: TERM=dumb"
TERM=dumb bv --robot-triage > /dev/null 2>&1 && log "TEST_4" "PASS: No crash in dumb terminal" || log "TEST_4" "FAIL: Crash in dumb terminal"
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All Go unit tests pass: go test ./pkg/ui/ -v -run TestColor
- Manual script exits 0 on all 4 modes
- Visual inspection: no unreadable text on Solarized Dark with TERM=xterm
- No regression: TrueColor rendering unchanged (screenshot comparison)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:36:44.477292394Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T19:26:43.534163924Z","external_ref":"gh:Dicklesworthstone/beads_viewer#101/test","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["color","terminal-compat","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2rih","depends_on_id":"bd-23lq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:36:49.867294615Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":69,"issue_id":"bd-2rih","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"MANUAL TEST SCOPE: The scripts/test_color_compat.sh script uses bv --robot-triage (JSON output) which does NOT test TUI color rendering. It tests crash-safety under different TERM settings. This is still valuable (verifying no panic from colorprofile.Detect() in different terminals) but does NOT verify visual correctness. For visual testing, a human must run bare 'bv' (interactive TUI) under TERM=xterm and visually inspect that backgrounds are transparent and text is readable. This is inherently manual and cannot be automated.","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:26:43Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2v50","title":"FEATURE: bv robot-docs JSON (capabilities + examples)","description":"Problem: robot-help is plain text and not machine-readable for agents.

- Output is stable JSON with top-level metadata (generated_at, data_hash, version).
- Works with `--format json|toon` and respects env BV_OUTPUT_FORMAT.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-25T10:11:55.341236131Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T02:23:38.004213375Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T02:23:38.004194901Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["agent-friendly","docs","robot"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2v50","depends_on_id":"bd-c5g","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-01-25T10:11:55.341236131Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-s8wz","title":"TOON: Add E2E shell script tests","description":"E2E verification script for TOON integration.

## File
`scripts/test_bv_toon.sh`

## Test Cases
1. Format flag tests (`--format json`, `--format toon`)
2. Env var tests (`BV_OUTPUT_FORMAT`, `TOON_DEFAULT_FORMAT`)
3. Precedence tests (CLI overrides env)
4. Round-trip verification (encode → decode → compare)
5. Token savings measurement (>= 40% target)
6. All 35+ robot commands work with TOON

## Script Template
```bash
#!/bin/bash
bv --robot-triage > /tmp/triage.json
bv --robot-triage --format toon > /tmp/triage.toon
tru --decode /tmp/triage.toon > /tmp/decoded.json
diff <(jq -S . /tmp/triage.json) <(jq -S . /tmp/decoded.json)
echo "Round-trip OK"
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T07:22:15.679571934Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T18:01:12.394667119Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T18:01:12.394648224Z","close_reason":"Test script completed and passing - test_toon_e2e.sh covers all requirements","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-t8dx","title":"Add release notes fallback workflow for manual releases (GH #99)","description":"## Context
GitHub issue #99 filed by qiviut (2026-02-06). Releases created by GoReleaser (via GH Actions) have full changelogs, but manually-created releases have no release notes. Example: v0.14.3 (GoReleaser) has notes, v0.14.4 (manual) has none.

## Owner Response
Confirmed the gap. When GoReleaser fails, binaries are uploaded manually via GitHub UI with minimal description. Proposed adding a fallback workflow triggered on release: [created] events.

## Current State
- Only workflow: .github/workflows/release.yml (triggers on push: tags: ['v*'], runs GoReleaser)
- GoReleaser config: .goreleaser.yaml (changelog: sort: asc, filters: exclude: ['^docs:', '^test:'])
- Beads bd-3v9s and bd-37ki created to track this, but NO actual workflow file was added
- Two commits (e0a1bd8, b50820a) only added beads entries, not implementation

## Implementation Plan
Create .github/workflows/release-notes-fallback.yml that:
1. Triggers on release: types: [created, edited]
2. Checks if release body is empty or minimal (< 50 characters)
3. Uses GitHub API: gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/releases/generate-notes -f tag_name=$TAG
4. Updates release body: gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/releases/{id} -X PATCH -f body="$NOTES"
5. Includes idempotency marker (<!-- auto-generated -->) so it doesn't overwrite manually written notes on subsequent edits

## Key Files
- NEW: .github/workflows/release-notes-fallback.yml
- EXISTING: .github/workflows/release.yml (reference for trigger patterns)
- EXISTING: .goreleaser.yaml (reference for changelog config)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:17:19.887335054Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T18:17:19.887335054Z","external_ref":"gh:Dicklesworthstone/beads_viewer#99","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","release","workflow"]}
+{"id":"bd-sfad","title":"Test: release notes fallback workflow correctness","description":"## What
Test the release-notes-fallback workflow (bd-t8dx) to verify it correctly populates empty releases.

## Test Plan

### Test 1: Workflow YAML validation
1. Run `gh workflow list -R Dicklesworthstone/beads_viewer` to verify workflow is registered
2. Validate YAML syntax: `python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open(\".github/workflows/release-notes-fallback.yml\"))"` 
3. Verify trigger is `on: release: types: [created, edited]`
4. Log: workflow name, triggers, jobs

### Test 2: Idempotency marker test
1. Create a draft release with body containing "<!-- auto-generated -->"
2. Trigger workflow (or simulate the logic)
3. Verify: body is NOT overwritten (idempotency marker detected)
4. Delete draft release
5. Log: release body before/after

### Test 3: Empty release gets notes
1. Create a draft release with empty body
2. Run the generate-notes API: `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/releases/generate-notes -f tag_name=v0.14.4`
3. Verify: non-empty notes returned
4. Delete draft release
5. Log: generated notes content, character count

### Test 4: Existing notes are preserved
1. Create a draft release with body "Manual release notes here"
2. Verify: workflow would NOT overwrite (body length > 50 chars)
3. Delete draft release
4. Log: body content, skip reason

## Acceptance Criteria
- YAML is valid and workflow registers
- Idempotency prevents double-write
- Empty releases get auto-generated notes
- Manual releases are not overwritten","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:37:07.112634091Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T18:37:11.747019517Z","external_ref":"gh:Dicklesworthstone/beads_viewer#99/test","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","release","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-sfad","depends_on_id":"bd-t8dx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:37:11.746934358Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-xgf7","title":"TOON: Create tru binary wrapper (pkg/robot/toon.go)","description":"Create Go wrapper for toon_rust tru binary.

## File
`pkg/robot/toon.go`

## Implementation
Use shell-out pattern from /data/projects/templates/toon_go_template.go:

```go
func encodeToon(payload any) (string, error) {
    jsonBytes, err := json.Marshal(payload)
    if err != nil {
        return "", err
    }
    trPath, err := getToonBinary()
    if err != nil {
        return "", err
    }
    cmd := exec.Command(trPath)
    cmd.Stdin = bytes.NewReader(jsonBytes)
    // ...
}
```

## Dependencies
- toon_rust tru binary must be installed
- TOON_TRU_BIN env var for custom path","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T07:16:31.960405372Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T07:06:17.263276562Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T07:06:17.263256955Z","close_reason":"Already implemented in cmd/bv/main.go using toon-go package - toonRobotEncoder struct handles encoding","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
